Gym has been unmaintained since 2022 and does not support NumPy 2.0 amongst other critical functionality.
Please upgrade to Gymnasium, the maintained drop-in replacement of Gym, or contact the authors of your software and request that they upgrade.
Users of this version of Gym should be able to simply replace 'import gym' with 'import gymnasium as gym' in the vast majority of cases.
See the migration guide at https://gymnasium.farama.org/introduction/migration_guide/ for additional information.

A value is trying to be set on a copy of a slice from a DataFrame.

Gym has been unmaintained since 2022 and does not support NumPy 2.0 amongst other critical functionality.
Please upgrade to Gymnasium, the maintained drop-in replacement of Gym, or contact the authors of your software and request that they upgrade.
Users of this version of Gym should be able to simply replace 'import gym' with 'import gymnasium as gym' in the vast majority of cases.
See the migration guide at https://gymnasium.farama.org/introduction/migration_guide/ for additional information.

2026-03-11 10:55:46.216 | INFO     | quantaalpha.pipeline.loop:__init__:89 - Initialized AlphaAgentLoop, backtest in local
2026-03-11 10:55:46.217 | INFO     | quantaalpha.pipeline.loop:__init__:91 - Initial direction: Hypothesis: Momentum is not a uniform linear process but exhibits regime-dependent acceleration. Test the 'Momentum Acceleration' factor by calculating the ratio of short-term returns (e.g., 1-month) to long-term returns (e.g., 12-month) excluding the most recent month. A ratio greater than 1 signals accelerating momentum, suggesting stronger persistent demand, while a ratio less than 1 signals decelerating momentum, potentially indicating trend exhaustion. This orthogonal approach focuses on the curvature of the price trajectory rather than the slope.
